generally admired

(often used as a combining form) in a good or proper or satisfactory manner or to a high standard ('good' is a nonstandard dialectal variant for 'well')

Synonyms

  • well

Related Words

  • combining form

completely and absolutely ('good' is sometimes used informally for 'thoroughly')

Synonyms

  • soundly
  • thoroughly

Related Words

  • colloquialism

References in classic literature ?
And in that case they will be right in doing good to the evil and evil to the good?
But the good are just and would not do an injustice?
Then I suppose that we ought to do good to the just and harm to the unjust?
View in context
SOCRATES: Do we not say that virtue is a good?--This is a hypothesis which is not set aside.
SOCRATES: Now, if there be any sort of good which is distinct from knowledge, virtue may be that good; but if knowledge embraces all good, then we shall be right in thinking that virtue is knowledge?
SOCRATES: And if we are good, then we are profitable; for all good things are profitable?
View in context
Good went next, and I came last, carrying the basket, and on reaching the bottom lit one of the two remaining matches.
Now we were quite near it, and Good, who was leading, swore that he could smell it.
"Go gently, Good," said Sir Henry, "we must be close." /Splash!/ and a cry from Good.
View in context
He was young, and had a bad name for shying and starting, by which he had lost a good place.
"I was timid when I was young, and was a good deal frightened several times, and if I saw anything strange I used to turn and look at it -- you see, with our blinkers one can't see or understand what a thing is unless one looks round -- and then my master always gave me a whipping, which of course made me start on, and did not make me less afraid.
View in context
"Ha!" cried Robin, "how likest thou that shot, good fellow?
So, in all that year, fivescore or more good stout yeomen gathered about Robin Hood, and chose him to be their leader and chief.
View in context
"Good Sir Steward," said Little John, "give me to dine, for it is long for Greenleaf to be fasting."
Placing these down on a convenient shelf he fell to with right good will.
View in context
